title: Branchwater Web UI (bw ui) — visual snapshots, branches & cross-branch DB diff
overview: A local web UI for Branchwater. `bw ui` starts a localhost HTTP server (built at the composition root, reusing the existing Orchestrator) serving a React + Vite + Tailwind app where a developer runs every version-control op (snapshot/branch/checkout/delete, destructive ones gated by a confirm dialog), browses each engine's tables with row counts and paginated previews, and diffs database contents across branches. The core stays engine-agnostic via a new OPTIONAL adapter capability (introspection + scratch-DB materialization); engines that can't introspect opt out.
scopeChallenge: EXPANSION confirmed with the user. Reuses orchestrator/adapter/manifest heavily but adds a large net-new surface (local server, React frontend, new adapter capability, complex scratch-DB diff), warranting comprehensive tests. The cross-branch diff is the riskiest/heaviest piece and stays in this plan but in later DAG layers (server + table browser are usable first). Confirmed: local web app (not TUI/desktop); max data depth (tables + counts + preview + cross-branch diff); engine-agnostic optional InspectableAdapter/MaterializableAdapter capabilities; boundary rule extended to src/server/**. Ruled out: writing/editing DB rows from the UI (read-only), auth beyond localhost+token, remote/multi-user hosting, a second adapter, realtime push. Structure: the strict-CJS Node backend and the ESM/Vite React frontend are different build systems, so the frontend is a new npm workspace at web/; the server uses Node built-in http (no Express) and serves the built web assets.

failureModes.risk: Engine specifics leak into the new server layer
failureModes.mitigation: Server talks only to the Orchestrator + capability guards; eslint + arch test extended to src/server/** (TASK-027)

failureModes.risk: Capability methods called on an engine that lacks them
failureModes.mitigation: isInspectable/isMaterializable guards (TASK-001) + typed 'not supported' errors; proven with a fake adapter (TASK-025)

failureModes.risk: Scratch DB leaked after a failed diff
failureModes.mitigation: dispose() in finally; materialize drops a half-created DB on restore failure; asserted in TASK-025/028

failureModes.risk: SQL injection via table/column identifiers in preview
failureModes.mitigation: Quote all identifiers; never string-interpolate names; injection test (TASK-024)

failureModes.risk: Local web server reachable by other users / CSRF from a browser
failureModes.mitigation: Bind 127.0.0.1 only + per-session token required on /api/*; token test (TASK-026)

failureModes.risk: Destructive op triggered from the UI by accident
failureModes.mitigation: Server requires {confirm:true} on checkout/delete; UI gates them behind a confirm dialog

failureModes.risk: Snapshot inspection summary slows or breaks snapshot
failureModes.mitigation: Best-effort + non-fatal; omitted on error; snapshot still succeeds

failureModes.risk: Unbounded row preview / diff payloads (DoS / memory)
failureModes.mitigation: Cap/validate limit/offset; bound row-level diff size; 413 on oversized request bodies (TASK-011)

failureModes.risk: Two build systems (CJS server vs ESM/Vite web) collide
failureModes.mitigation: Separate web/ workspace + tsconfig; type-only @bw/dto sharing; build copies web/dist -> dist/web

failureModes.risk: Manifest back-compat broken by the new inspection field
failureModes.mitigation: Field is optional; old manifests validate; back-compat asserted

failureModes.risk: Full cross-branch diff materializes an entire snapshot into a scratch DB — slow/large for big databases
failureModes.mitigation: Count/schema diff from manifest summaries is the cheap default; full row-level diff is opt-in and bounded; consider a timeout on materialize

testCoverageMap.codepath: Postgres table-list / preview SQL + identifier quoting
Postgres materialize: scratch DB create/restore/drop + dispose
Snapshot records inspection summary (best-effort) + manifest back-compat
Real PG inspect + materialize + diff row delta
Auth token rejection + destructive confirm gate

tasks.title: Adapter introspection & materialization capability types
tasks.description: Extend src/core/adapter/types.ts with OPTIONAL capability interfaces + value types: ColumnInfo, TableRef, TableInfo (name, schema?, rowCount: number|null, columns), EngineInspection (tables), TablePage (columns, rows, total, offset, limit), InspectableAdapter (inspect, previewTable), MaterializableAdapter (materialize -> MaterializedSnapshot), MaterializedSnapshot (context: AdapterContext, dispose()), and runtime guards isInspectable/isMaterializable. Pure types + guards, no engine imports.
acceptanceCriteria: All capability interfaces + guards compile under strict mode with zero adapter imports
isInspectable/isMaterializable narrow an object lacking the methods to false
EngineAdapter itself is unchanged (capabilities are separate, opt-in interfaces)

tasks.title: HTTP API DTO types
tasks.description: Create src/server/dto.ts with request/response shapes for every endpoint: StateDTO, SnapshotReqDTO, BranchReqDTO, CheckoutReqDTO ({name, confirm:true}), DeleteReqDTO, EngineListDTO (name/type/inspectable), TableListDTO, TablePageDTO, BranchDiffDTO (table-level + row-level deltas), and a shared ApiError. Reuse TASK-001 table/column types. Plain types only.
acceptanceCriteria: Every endpoint in the Architecture has a request and/or response DTO
BranchDiffDTO represents added/removed tables, per-table row-count delta, and schema changes
DTOs reuse TASK-001 TableInfo/ColumnInfo rather than redefining them

tasks.title: Manifest snapshot inspection summary (schema + types)
tasks.description: Extend src/core/manifest/types.ts and schema.ts: add OPTIONAL inspection?: Record<string, EngineInspectionSummary> to SnapshotRecord (engine -> tables with name/schema/rowCount/columns, NO rows). Update zod schema with the optional field; existing manifests without it must still validate.
acceptanceCriteria: A record with an inspection summary validates; one without it still validates (back-compat)
Summary carries table names, row counts, and column schema but never row data
Types and zod schema stay structurally aligned (existing alignment guard compiles)

tasks.title: npm workspace + web/ package scaffold
tasks.description: Add workspaces:[web] to root package.json. Create web/package.json (react@19, react-dom@19; devDeps vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, typescript, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, vitest, @testing-library/react, jsdom) and web/tsconfig.json (strict, jsx react-jsx, paths alias @bw/dto -> ../src/server/dto for type-only imports). Keep web tsconfig independent of the root CJS tsconfig.
acceptanceCriteria: npm install resolves the workspace and web deps without touching the CLI runtime deps
web/tsconfig.json is strict and independent of the root CJS tsconfig
A type-only import from @bw/dto resolves in the web package

tasks.title: Vite + Tailwind config and HTML entry
tasks.description: Create web/vite.config.ts (React plugin; dev server.proxy /api -> bw server; build.outDir dist), web/tailwind.config.js + web/postcss.config.js, and web/index.html (mounts #root, loads src/main.tsx, reads the server-injected session token). Tailwind scans index.html + src/**.
acceptanceCriteria: npm -w web run build produces web/dist/index.html + hashed assets (against a placeholder main.tsx entry)
Tailwind utility classes apply in a build
Dev server proxies /api/* so the SPA can call the bw server in dev

tasks.title: Postgres introspection SQL builders
tasks.description: Create src/adapters/postgres/introspect.ts: build + run (via util/exec/psql) queries against information_schema/pg_catalog to list user tables with row counts and column schema, and previewTable using safely quoted identifiers + LIMIT/OFFSET. Return TASK-001 EngineInspection/TablePage shapes. No secrets in argv (reuse buildPgEnv).
acceptanceCriteria: Table-list query is restricted to user schemas (excludes pg_catalog/information_schema)
previewTable quotes identifiers (a table named '"a"; drop' cannot inject SQL)
Row-count uses an estimate or null rather than a blocking full scan when unavailable

tasks.title: PostgresAdapter implements InspectableAdapter
tasks.description: Modify src/adapters/postgres/index.ts so PostgresAdapter implements InspectableAdapter (inspect, previewTable) delegating to introspect.ts, normalizing via normalizePgConnection. Keep PG specifics inside the adapter.
acceptanceCriteria: isInspectable(new PostgresAdapter()) is true
inspect() returns tables with counts; previewTable() honors limit/offset
previewTable() on a non-existent table rejects with a clear error (no silent empty page)

tasks.title: PostgresAdapter implements MaterializableAdapter (scratch DB)
tasks.description: Create src/adapters/postgres/materialize.ts and implement MaterializableAdapter in index.ts. materialize(ctx,id) creates a uniquely-named scratch DB (bw_scratch_<rand>), pg_restores the snapshot .dump into it, returns a MaterializedSnapshot whose context points at the scratch DB plus dispose() that DROPs it (terminating backends first). Reuse pgtools.
acceptanceCriteria: materialize() returns a context addressing the scratch DB; inspect() on it works
dispose() drops the scratch DB and is idempotent (second call does not throw)
A pg_restore failure during materialize still drops the half-created scratch DB (no leak)

tasks.title: Orchestrator inspection methods
tasks.description: Modify src/core/orchestrator.ts: add inspectEngine(name) and previewTable(name, table, opts) that resolve the adapter via registry, narrow with isInspectable (TASK-001), and call through, throwing a clear 'engine does not support inspection' error otherwise. Import only capability interfaces, never an adapter.
acceptanceCriteria: inspectEngine returns tables for an inspectable adapter
A non-inspectable engine yields a clear typed error (not a TypeError on a missing method)
orchestrator.ts still imports nothing from src/adapters/**

tasks.title: Snapshot records an inspection summary (best-effort)
tasks.description: Modify src/core/orchestrator.ts snapshot path: after each engine snapshot, if the adapter is inspectable, capture a lightweight summary (table names + counts + schema) onto SnapshotRecord.inspection (TASK-003). Best-effort: a failed/absent inspection must NOT fail the snapshot.
acceptanceCriteria: A snapshot over an inspectable engine records its table/count summary
A non-inspectable engine produces a valid snapshot with no inspection entry
An inspection error during snapshot is swallowed (logged) and the snapshot still succeeds

tasks.title: HTTP router + static file serving util
tasks.description: Create src/server/http.ts: a dependency-free router over Node http (method+path+params), JSON body parsing with a size cap, sendJson/sendError helpers, and a static-file handler with index.html SPA fallback and content-types. No external deps.
acceptanceCriteria: Routes match by method + path params; unknown routes return 404 JSON
A request body over the size cap is rejected with 413 rather than buffered unbounded
Static handler serves index.html for unknown non-/api paths and 404s missing assets

tasks.title: Localhost bind + per-session auth token
tasks.description: Create src/server/security.ts: generate a random session token, a guard that rejects /api/* requests missing the token (header or query) with 401, and a helper asserting a 127.0.0.1 bind. Expose the token so the served HTML can embed it.
acceptanceCriteria: An /api request without the valid token gets 401; with it, passes through
The token is unguessable (>=128 bits of randomness)
Static asset/index requests do not require the token (page can bootstrap)

tasks.title: Ops endpoints (state + snapshot/branch/checkout/delete)
tasks.description: Create src/server/routes/ops.ts: GET /api/state (orchestrator.list()), POST /api/snapshot|branch|checkout|delete calling the injected orchestrator. checkout/delete REQUIRE {confirm:true} and return the orchestrator result (e.g. checkout autosaveId). Uses TASK-002 DTOs.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/state returns the manifest (branches, snapshots, head) as JSON
POST /api/checkout without confirm:true returns 400 and does NOT touch any database
An orchestrator error maps to a 4xx JSON ApiError, not a 500 stack

tasks.title: Introspection endpoints (engines, tables, row preview)
tasks.description: Create src/server/routes/inspect.ts: GET /api/engines (name/type/inspectable), GET /api/engines/:name/tables (orchestrator.inspectEngine), GET /api/engines/:name/tables/:table?limit&offset (orchestrator.previewTable). Validate/cap limit and offset. Uses TASK-002 DTOs.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/engines flags which engines are inspectable
Tables/preview endpoints return TASK-002 DTO shapes; preview honors limit/offset
A limit above the cap (or non-numeric) is clamped/rejected rather than passed through unbounded

tasks.title: Server assembly + bw ui command
tasks.description: Create src/server/server.ts (createBwServer({orchestrator, webDir}) wiring router + ops + inspect + static + auth; returns {url, token, close()}, binds 127.0.0.1) and src/cli/commands/ui.ts (registers bw ui [--port][--no-open], builds the orchestrator from injected deps, starts server, prints tokenized URL, optionally opens the browser). Register the command in src/cli/index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: bw ui starts a server on 127.0.0.1 and prints a URL containing the session token
--port 0 picks a free port; --no-open does not launch a browser
src/server/** and src/cli/commands/ui.ts import nothing from src/adapters/**

tasks.title: Web app shell + typed API client
tasks.description: Replace the placeholder web/src/main.tsx (from TASK-005) to mount the real app reading the window token; create web/src/App.tsx (sidebar + main panel layout) and web/src/api.ts (typed fetch client attaching the token, parsing ApiError). Type-only imports from @bw/dto (TASK-002 via TASK-004 alias).
acceptanceCriteria: The app renders a shell and calls GET /api/state through the client
The API client attaches the session token and surfaces ApiError.message on failures
A failed/non-JSON response is handled without crashing the app (error state shown)

tasks.title: Branch list + snapshot graph sidebar
tasks.description: Create web/src/components/BranchList.tsx and web/src/components/SnapshotGraph.tsx: render branches (mark HEAD), snapshots with parent lineage, from /api/state. Selecting a branch sets the active branch in app state.
acceptanceCriteria: Branches render with the current HEAD visually distinguished
Snapshots render newest-first with parent lineage
An empty repo renders an empty state, not a crash

tasks.title: Action bar + confirm dialog
tasks.description: Create web/src/components/Actions.tsx (snapshot w/ message, create branch w/ name) and web/src/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx (modal). Checkout/delete go through the confirm dialog and post {confirm:true}; on success refetch /api/state and surface the checkout autosave id.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshot/branch actions call the API and refresh state on success
Checkout/delete are blocked until the confirm dialog is accepted
An API error from an action is shown to the user (not swallowed)

tasks.title: Engine table browser + row preview
tasks.description: Create web/src/components/TableBrowser.tsx (per-engine table list with row counts) and web/src/components/TablePreview.tsx (paginated read-only rows with column headers/types). Drives /api/engines, /tables, /tables/:table. Non-inspectable engines show a 'browsing not supported' note.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting an engine lists its tables with row counts; selecting a table previews rows
Pagination updates offset and re-fetches
A non-inspectable engine renders the unsupported note instead of erroring

tasks.title: Frontend data-fetching hooks
tasks.description: Create web/src/hooks/useApi.ts: small typed hooks wrapping the API client with loading/error/refetch state (no heavy data library), used by the views; centralizes refetch-after-mutation.
acceptanceCriteria: A hook exposes {data, loading, error, refetch} and ignores stale responses
Mutations can trigger a coordinated refetch of dependent queries
An unmounted component does not set state after an in-flight request resolves

tasks.title: Orchestrator diffBranches (counts/schema + scratch-DB row diff)
tasks.description: Modify src/core/orchestrator.ts: add diffBranches(from, to). Use manifest inspection summaries (TASK-003/010) for instant table/count/schema diff when present; for full row-level diff, materialize (TASK-001 guard) the target snapshot into a scratch context, inspect/previewTable both sides, compute per-table deltas, then dispose() always, even on error. Engine-agnostic (capability guards only).
acceptanceCriteria: Count/schema diff is returned from manifest summaries without materializing when both exist
Full diff materializes the target, computes row deltas, and dispose()s the scratch resource
If materialize/diff throws, dispose() still runs (no leaked scratch DB) and a clear error propagates

tasks.title: Diff endpoint
tasks.description: Create src/server/routes/diff.ts (GET /api/diff?from=&to= -> orchestrator.diffBranches, returning BranchDiffDTO) and mount it in src/server/server.ts. Validate both branches exist; bound row-level diff size.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/diff?from=main&to=exp returns a BranchDiffDTO
Missing/unknown from/to yields 400, not a 500
Row-level diff output is bounded (capped), not an unbounded payload

tasks.title: Cross-branch diff view
tasks.description: Create web/src/components/DiffView.tsx and web/src/components/TableDiff.tsx: choose two branches, show table-level diff (added/removed tables, row-count deltas, schema changes), drill into a table's row-level diff. Drives /api/diff.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting two branches renders the table-level diff with clear add/remove/changed markers
Drilling into a changed table shows row-level differences
Diffing a branch against itself shows 'no differences' (not an error)

tasks.title: Unit tests — Postgres introspection + materialize (mocked exec)
tasks.description: Create test/adapters/postgres-introspect.test.ts and test/adapters/postgres-materialize.test.ts, mocking the exec/psql layer. Introspect: table-list targets user schemas, previewTable quotes identifiers + LIMIT/OFFSET, an injection-y table name can't break out. Materialize: creates a uniquely-named scratch DB, restores into it, dispose() drops it (idempotent; drops a half-created DB on restore failure).
acceptanceCriteria: Asserts table-list excludes system schemas and previewTable quotes identifiers + binds limit/offset
A malicious table name ('; DROP TABLE x; --) is safely quoted, not interpolated raw
Asserts materialize creates+restores a scratch DB and dispose() drops it (idempotent, incl. after a restore failure)

tasks.title: Unit tests — orchestrator inspect/diff with a fake capability adapter
tasks.description: Create test/core/orchestrator-inspect.test.ts: a FakeInspectableAdapter implementing InspectableAdapter + MaterializableAdapter in memory. Proves the new paths are engine-agnostic with NO Postgres: inspect/preview, a non-inspectable engine error, and diffBranches (incl. that dispose() is called).
acceptanceCriteria: inspect/previewTable work end-to-end via the fake adapter (no Postgres)
A non-inspectable engine produces the typed 'not supported' error
diffBranches computes deltas and the fake dispose() is invoked exactly once (even on a forced error)

A snapshot over the fake inspectable adapter records the inspection summary on the SnapshotRecord; a forced inspection error is swallowed and the snapshot still succeeds (covers TASK-010); a manifest without the summary still loads (covers TASK-003 back-compat)

tasks.title: Server endpoint tests
tasks.description: Create test/server/server.test.ts: boot createBwServer with a fake orchestrator on an ephemeral port; assert /api/state, ops (incl. checkout requiring confirm), inspection, and diff endpoints; token rejection (401) and SPA static fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: Endpoints return the expected DTO JSON against a fake orchestrator
A request without the token gets 401; checkout without confirm gets 400
An unknown non-/api path serves index.html; an unknown /api path returns 404 JSON

tasks.title: Extend engine-agnostic boundary to src/server/**
tasks.description: Modify eslint.config.js (add src/server/**/*.ts to the no-restricted-imports scope forbidding src/adapters/**) and test/arch/agnostic.test.ts (scan src/server too, still exempting src/cli/index.ts).
acceptanceCriteria: eslint errors if a src/server file imports src/adapters/**
The arch test scans src/server and passes against the real tree
src/cli/index.ts remains the sole exempt composition root

tasks.title: Gated integration test — real Postgres introspect + materialize + diff
tasks.description: Create test/integration/postgres-inspect.int.test.ts (gated on BW_TEST_PG_URL, skips when unset): snapshot a seeded DB, mutate, snapshot again, then assert inspect counts, a materialize round trip, and a diffBranches showing the row delta; clean up the scratch DB.
acceptanceCriteria: With a real DB, inspect reports correct counts and diff shows the inserted-row delta
The scratch database created by materialize is dropped after the test
The suite SKIPS (not fails) when BW_TEST_PG_URL is unset

tasks.title: Frontend component tests (vitest + testing-library)
tasks.description: Create web/vitest.config.ts and web/src/components/__tests__/components.test.tsx: render TableBrowser/TablePreview, ConfirmDialog (gates the destructive action), and DiffView (renders deltas) against a mocked API client.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmDialog does not fire its action until confirmed
TableBrowser renders tables/counts from a mocked response and a non-inspectable note
DiffView renders added/removed/changed markers from a mocked diff

tasks.title: bw ui boot e2e
tasks.description: Create test/e2e/ui.test.ts: spawn bw ui --no-open --port 0 in a seeded workspace, parse the printed URL+token, GET /api/state (200 with token, 401 without), confirm index.html is served, then shut the server down.
acceptanceCriteria: The server boots, serves index.html, and /api/state returns 200 with the token
/api/state without the token returns 401
The spawned process exits cleanly on teardown (no leaked port/process)

tasks.title: Build pipeline (bundle the web app into dist)
tasks.description: Modify root package.json build script and add scripts/build-web.mjs: run vite build for the workspace and copy web/dist -> dist/web so bw ui serves it from the published package; ensure dist/web and web/dist are git-ignored. Server resolves webDir to dist/web (prod) or proxies in dev.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build produces both dist/cli/index.js and dist/web/index.html
A built bw ui serves the bundled assets from dist/web
Build artifacts (dist, web/dist) are git-ignored

tasks.title: CI — build + test the workspace
tasks.description: Modify .github/workflows/ci.yml: install workspaces, run root lint/typecheck/test + build, and the web build + vitest run; keep gated PG tests opt-in so CI is green without a DB.
acceptanceCriteria: CI installs workspaces and runs both backend and web test suites
CI builds the web bundle as part of the pipeline
Gated PG inspect/integration tests are skipped (not failed) without a database

tasks.title: Docs — Web UI guide + adapter capabilities
tasks.description: Update README.md (a Web UI section: bw ui, the localhost+token model, the table browser + cross-branch diff) and docs/ADAPTERS.md (document the OPTIONAL InspectableAdapter/MaterializableAdapter capabilities and that omitting them is fine).
acceptanceCriteria: README documents bw ui, its flags, and the security model (localhost + session token)
ADAPTERS.md documents both optional capabilities and that they are opt-in
Docs state that destructive ops in the UI require confirmation
